name: list

schema.description: When ``true``, narrow the result to the caller's own claims (equivalent to ``user_id=<caller>``) without requiring ``expenses.approve``. Mutually exclusive with an explicit ``user_id`` — supplying both surfaces 422 ``mine_user_id_conflict``. Defaults to ``false``.
schema.title: Mine

schema.description: Opaque forward cursor from the previous page's ``next_cursor``. Omitted on the first call. Bounded to 256 chars to keep the URL below reverse-proxy header limits.

schema.description: Maximum rows to return. Default 50, cap 500 per spec §12 'Pagination'. Rejected with 422 outside ``[1, 500]``.
schema.title: Limit

summary: Manager queue — submitted claims awaiting decision

name: pending-reimbursement

schema.description: Filter target. ``me`` resolves to the caller (no capability required). A different user id requires ``expenses.approve``. Omit the param entirely to get the workspace-wide aggregate (also gated on ``expenses.approve``); the response then carries a ``by_user`` breakdown.
schema.title: User Id

summary: Pending-reimbursement totals (per-user or workspace aggregate)
